notes: Satellites do not map culturally defined trails. Use AOIs as hunting/travel *regions* and indices as *proxies* (snow, wetness, vegetation).
Sentinel-2 optical data need daylight and clear sky. Winter storms (blizzards) require SAR (e.g. Sentinel-1) or reanalysis, not S2.
Each entry includes public references for your write-up; verify details before citing in formal work.

events.title: Warm May–June 2023 vs 1991–2020 baseline, Kugluktuk / Coronation Gulf

sentinel2.reason: Spring daylight; snow–melt contrast on land and nearshore.

events.title: Exceptionally warm July 2022, Nunavut (Iqaluit-area surface conditions — verify climate at this grid point)

sentinel2.reason: Mid-summer daylight; compare early vs late July green-up / snow patch loss after record-warm month.

events.title: Severe weather / infrastructure stress, Pangnirtung area, December 2023

sentinel2.reason: December polar night at this latitude: no useful Sentinel-2 surface reflectance. Use ERA5/reanalysis wind & precip, ground reports, and/or Sentinel-1 SAR if you implement it.

events.title: Record Arctic cyclone, January 2022 (sea-ice pack, not community trails)

sentinel2.reason: Peak impacts were over the ice pack (e.g. Barents/Kara sector) under winter darkness; science papers use microwave sea ice, waves, and reanalysis. Not a drop-in S2 trail case study.
